task_completion.description: Verify the target directory was created
task_completion.visual_evidence: A file manager window or terminal output showing the 'Workshop_Materials' directory exists inside the 'Documents' folder.

task_completion.description: Verify Bloom's Taxonomy PDF is saved
task_completion.visual_evidence: The file 'blooms_taxonomy.pdf' is visible in the 'Workshop_Materials' directory via a file manager or terminal list command.
The file 'constructivism.pdf' is visible in the 'Workshop_Materials' directory via a file manager or terminal list command.

task_completion.description: Verify Differentiated Instruction PDF is saved
task_completion.visual_evidence: The file 'differentiated_instruction.pdf' is visible in the 'Workshop_Materials' directory via a file manager or terminal list command.

task_completion.description: Verify the README.txt index file is created
task_completion.visual_evidence: The file 'README.txt' is visible in the 'Workshop_Materials' directory alongside the PDF files.

task_completion.description: Verify the README.txt contains the required filenames and descriptions
task_completion.visual_evidence: A text editor or terminal output (e.g., cat command) displaying the contents of 'README.txt'. The text must explicitly list the three PDF filenames and include a brief description of each topic (Bloom's Taxonomy, Constructivism, and Differentiated Instruction).

integrity.description: Verify the agent used a web browser to generate the PDFs
integrity.visual_evidence: Screenshots should show a web browser (like Firefox or Chromium) navigating to the Wikipedia articles and using the 'Print to PDF' or 'Save as PDF' GUI dialog, rather than just using terminal commands to download HTML files.

integrity.description: Verify the PDFs are actual documents and not empty or renamed HTML files
integrity.visual_evidence: File manager properties, terminal output (e.g., ls -lh) showing file sizes > 20 KB, or a PDF viewer opened to show the actual formatted Wikipedia content.

integrity.description: Verify the agent interacted with a text editor to write the README
integrity.visual_evidence: Screenshots showing a GUI text editor (like gedit) or a terminal-based editor (like nano/vim) being used to type or paste the descriptions into README.txt.

privileged_info_for_vlm: The target directory must be exactly /home/ga/Documents/Workshop_Materials. The required files are blooms_taxonomy.pdf, constructivism.pdf, differentiated_instruction.pdf, and README.txt. Each PDF must be a genuine PDF conversion of the respective Wikipedia article, which means each file should be at least 20 KB (20480 bytes) in size. The README.txt must contain the three filenames and a brief description for each of the three educational concepts.
